## Release — backlog-campaign v0.14.0

### ✨ What's new in v0.14.0

Minor release: **the campaign's "thinking" workflows go autonomous** (ADR-010). Where doing
workflows (plan → implement → review) already ran unattended, the thinking routes — analysis,
design, brainstorming, retrospection — previously required a human at every step. This release
closes that gap with confidence-gated routing and a deterministic design verdict, all behind an
opt-in `autonomy` config block that defaults off: absent or `enabled: false` preserves current
behavior exactly.

#### Autonomous thinking routes (ADR-010, M1–M4)

- **Confidence kernel + opt-in `autonomy` config** — the router now emits per-flag confidence
  (`brainstorm`, `analysis`) with cautious low-confidence defaults, and every dependent feature
  is a strict no-op unless `autonomy.enabled` is set. Durable artifact contract: thinking-phase
  outputs (analyses, brainstorms, ADRs) ship inside issue PRs, so merge = approval. (#295)
- **Design autonomy — blind critics + deterministic verdict** — the design track's unconditional
  human gate is replaced (opt-in) by a fixed per-decision-type rubric, two blind critic scorers,
  and `design-aggregate.ts`: a design only reaches `ready` when one option dominates by more than
  the configured margin under **all three** scorers with no CRITICAL discriminating finding —
  any anomaly fails closed to `blocked` (`V-AUTO-01`, `V-DESIGN-02`). (#297)
- **Investigator `analyze` sub-mode** — a new `needs_analysis` route flag (confidence-gated,
  cautious default for `size:l`+ / design-flagged issues) dispatches read-only
  conventions/architecture/performance evidence gathering before planning. (#298)
- **Planner `brainstorm` track** — generative expansion route: a `needs_brainstorm` issue is
  never planned directly; the planner produces a brainstorm artifact and child issues instead,
  with an enforced children cap. (#299, #305)

#### Kaizen

- **Retrospective hunt kind (M5)** — a new hunt wave that synthesizes recently merged PR metadata
  into candidate `needs_design` findings, with the same CONFIRMED-verification and per-wave caps
  as every other kind. (#296)

#### Worker contract validation

- **`validate-worker-json.ts`** — structural validation of every worker role's JSON output
  (planner, implementer, reviewer, router, investigator) at the handoff boundary, with fixtures
  for each role and sub-mode; malformed output is rejected before any queue/ledger mutation.

#### CI & security

- **CI on self-hosted runner, with a hardened trust boundary** — verify/release jobs moved to a
  self-hosted runner (#291); `pull_request`-triggered runs are pinned to GitHub-hosted
  `ubuntu-latest` so fork PRs can never execute code on the self-hosted machine.
- **Design-verdict hardening** — `design-aggregate.ts` now blocks when any scorer omits a rubric
  column for any option; a missing column previously scored as 0 and could flip a verdict.

---

**Full feature overview:** see [v0.1.0 release notes](https://github.com/CorentinLumineau/blackhole/releases/tag/v0.1.0) · [README](https://github.com/CorentinLumineau/blackhole#readme)
